export const _frontmatter = {};

const MDXLayout = DefaultLayout;
export default function MDXContent({

  return <MDXLayout {...layoutProps} {...props} components={components} mdxType="MDXLayout">


    <h1 {...{
      "id": "basic-routing"
    }}>{`Basic Routing`}</h1>
    <p>{`MetaFox platform support restful api routes.`}</p>
    <p>{`Routing define the way platform resolve uri request info into controller action,
In most of case you will begin by define routes in your package by `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`routes/api.php`}</inlineCode></p>
    <p>{`This file re-use laravel framework routing system in package levels, for futher information read out `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://laravel.com/docs/9.x/routing"
      }}>{`laravel routing`}</a></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php
/**
 * File name packages/metafox/blog/src/routes/api.php
 * This file defines route that begin at /api/{ver}/ prefix
 *
 */

// define namespace of main controller api gateway
namespace MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api;

// use laravel routing
use Illuminate\\Support\\Facades\\Route;

/*
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *  API Routes
 * --------------------------------------------------------------------------
 *
 *  This file will be loaded by @link \\MetaFox\\Platform\\PackageManager::getApiRoutes()
 */

Route::group([
    'namespace'  => __NAMESPACE__, // define all of controller
    'middleware' => 'auth:api',
], function () {

    Route::patch('blog/sponsor/{id}', 'BlogController@sponsor');
    Route::patch('blog/feature/{id}', 'BlogController@feature');
    Route::patch('blog/approve/{id}', 'BlogController@approve');
    Route::put('blog/publish/{id}', 'BlogController@publish');

    Route::resource('blog', 'BlogController');
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "route-method"
    }}>{`Route Method`}</h2>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php
// define route with http GET request
Route::get($uri, $callback);

//define route with http POST request
Route::post($uri, $callback);

//define route with http PUT request
Route::put($uri, $callback);

// define route with http PATCH request
Route::patch($uri, $callback);

 //define route with http POST request
Route::delete($uri, $callback);

//define route with http OPTION request
Route::options($uri, $callback);
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`In above source code, \\$callback naming convention is `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ControllerClassName@methodName`}</inlineCode></p>
    <p>{`For example `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Route::patch('blog/sponsor/{id}', 'BlogController@sponsor');`}</inlineCode>{` will dispatch controller `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`BlogController`}</inlineCode>{` in namespace `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MetaFox\\Blog\\Http\\Controllers\\Api`}</inlineCode>{` with method `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`sponsor`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "route-group"
    }}>{`Route Group`}</h2>
    <p>{`To organize api routes for more readable, laravel support group of routes, using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Route::group`}</inlineCode>{` method.`}</p>
    <p>{`For examples, all route in the group have the same controller namespace, middleware and prefix.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php
Route::group([
    'namespace'  => __NAMESPACE__, // define all of controller
    'middleware' => 'auth:api',
    'prefix'=> 'blog'
], function () {
  // all sub route have namespace
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "route-resource"
    }}>{`Route Resource`}</h2>
    <p>{`Routing for RESTful api request has a short method Route::resouce($uri, $callback)`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php

Route::resource('blog', 'BlogController');

`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`// it is the same to`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php

Route::get('blog', 'BlogController@index');
Route::get('blog/{id}', 'BlogController@show');
Route::delete('blog/{id}', 'BlogController@destroy');
Route::post('blog/{id}', 'BlogController@store');
Route::put('blog/{id}', 'BlogController@update');
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "route-cache"
    }}>{`Route Cache`}</h2>
    <p>{`For optimizing performance, laravel framework keep all routes in route caches. To affected new routes run artisan`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-bash"
      }}>{`
# clear routes cache
php artisan route:cache

# checkout routes

php artisan route:list
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "route-parameters"
    }}>{`Route Parameters`}</h2>
    <p>{`Sometimes you will need to capture segments of the URI within your route. For example, you may need to capture a user's ID from the URL. You may do so by defining route parameters within `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`{id}`}</inlineCode>{`, if the parameters is optional and question mark '?' after id.`}</p>
    <p>{`For example`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php
// api.php
Route::get('/user/{id}', 'UserController@show');

Route::get('/user/info/{id?}', 'UserController@info');

// UserController.php

<?php

class UserController {

  // get id=1 for /user/1
  function show(int $id){
    //
  }

  // get id=1 for /user/info/1
  // id=null for /user/info
  function info(?int $id){

  }
}

`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`For complex example with required parameter `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`postId`}</inlineCode>{` and optional parameter `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`commentId`}</inlineCode></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-php"
      }}>{`<?php
Route::get('/user/{postId}/comments/{comemntId?}', 'CommentController@view');
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "api-versioning"
    }}>{`API Versioning`}</h2>
    <p>{`In real world, when you have a long time project with multiple released version, the platform wraps all routes define in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`api.php`}</inlineCode>{` within a prefix=`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/api/{ver}`}</inlineCode>{`, parameter `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`{ver}`}</inlineCode>{` will be passed to controller action to help us define correct response base on version.`}</p>
    <p><a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/backend/controller"
      }}>{`Next Chapter`}</a></p>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
